rationale.R1.6: The writing is generally clear and grammatically correct, but there are some instances of awkward phrasing and redundancy.
rationale.RX.1: The text does not explicitly state the confidence levels for each recommendation or identify any missing evidence.
rationale.RX.3: The description covers the main aspects of the multi-reader aggregation framework, but it could benefit from a more precise definition of the active set and how it relates to the input data.
rationale.RX.4: The text does not mention any potential harm that the framework might introduce or any steps taken to avoid such harm.
rationale.RX.5: The text does not provide details about the provenance of the different components of the framework, such as the source code, training data, or evaluation metrics.
